short_description: KONE Security Operations isolated device 5cg0372kzr.konenet.com (user Han Li) due to malware. Investigation may take 24-48 hours. ASKIT must assign to regional IT teams, advise users to keep devices powered, and provide spare laptops for CAT 1/2 sites. Onsite teams should follow updates from Cybersecurity.
description:
Subject: [KONE Cybersecurity Operations] Machine Isolation Notification
Email Body: 
received from: dolly.mahore@kone.com

Hello,

We are contacting you from KONE Security Operations.

A security alert was detected on device 5cg0372kzr.konenet.com, which is associated with user Han Li, and you have been identified as the user's manager and associated regional IT SDM.

Information about the alert:

Device name: 5cg0372kzr.konenet.com
User name: Han Li
The reason for isolation: Malware found on the device.

After initial investigation, the affected device was decided to be isolated from KONE corporate network. This is a mitigative action to minimize the impact of the threat detected on the affected device. Investigation might last for 24 to 48 hours max based on the complexity and amount of logs to be investigated.

Note to ASKIT: Please assign this notification Incident to impacted region specific Onsite teams queue. If any impacted user contacts ASKIT, refer the Host isolation Incident and inform that the host is isolated due to security reasons and will be released from Isolation once the needed mitigations are performed. Please instruct the user to keep the laptop running and plugged into power if possible.

Note to Onsite team: Provide spare laptops to the impacted users in CAT 1 and CAT 2 sites. For CAT 3, spare laptop can be provided based on the investigation updates from Cybersecurity team.


Regards,
Dolly Mahore
AskIT Analyst
E-Mail:dolly.mahore@kone.com
"Need Help? We're just a call or chat away!''

short_description: Tickets from Email channel were incorrectly routed to Ask IT India instead of dedicated support groups. Analysis of 45 tickets revealed issues: correct S/SO selected but routed wrongly, missing S/SO fields, or irrelevant S/SO selections. Team requested to review routing logic for fixes. (158 characters)
description:
Subject: Tickets from Email channel incorrectly routed to Ask IT India instead of dedicated support groups
Email Body: 
received from: sanay.joshi@kone.com

A sample analysis was conducted on 45 tickets created via the Email channel that were routed to the Ask IT India queue. Based on the review, several tickets should have been automatically routed to their respective dedicated support groups based on the configured Service (S) and Service Offering (SO).

The findings are categorized as follows:

- Correct S/SO selected by system but ticket landed in Ask IT instead of default assignment group -
The system correctly identified the Service and Service Offering, and these S/SOs have dedicated support groups configured. However, the tickets still landed in the Ask IT India queue instead of the respective default assignment groups.

- No S/SO selected by system -
In some cases, the Service and Service Offering fields were not populated by the system.

- Irrelevant S/SO selected by system and ticket landed in Ask IT -
The system selected an S/SO that was not relevant to the ticket description. Additionally, even though those S/SOs also had dedicated support groups configured, the tickets still landed in the Ask IT India queue.

A detailed list of the analyzed tickets, along with observations, has been attached for reference. Requesting the team to review the routing logic for the Email channel and advise on possible corrections.


Regards,

Sanay Joshi

Ask IT SD Team Lead



For immediate assistance, please contact through:

  *   MS Teams AskIT Now Chat available 24/7
  *   AskIT Portal<http://askit.kone.com/> to log Incidents or Service Requests for expedited resolution

       [cid:841ea472-783e-43a1-90fe-af4f421bc9c0]

short_description: Enhancement Request: Track Completed Dates for Tickets
description: Currently, we can only extract the closed date details from ServiceNow, which appears three days after a ticket is completed when the status changes from Pending Confirmation to Closed. This makes it difficult for us to track how many tickets each agent completes per day in productivity reports, as we are missing the actual completed date for each ticket. Please enhance this process to provide better visibility.

short_description: User reports Teams issue: can send messages in office but cannot send when connected remotely (branch/remote work). Requests profile check to identify cause. (Issue: remote sending failure; request for investigation.)
description:
Subject: User reports Teams issue: can send messages in office but cannot send when connected remotely (branch/remote work). Requests profile check to identify cause. (1
Email Body: 
received from: alex.antony@kone.com

Good morning.
I'm having an issue with Teams: when I am in the office I can send text messages without any problems, but when I'm connected from another location (a branch office or working remotely), I can receive messages on Teams but I'm not able to send them.
Could you please check my profile to understand why Teams is giving me problems when I'm working remotely?
Thank you in advance.
BR
Alex

Kiran
